table.name: damages
table.remarks: Damage to prison property recorded on a draft adjudication. Copied to reported_damages on submission.

column.name: draft_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to draft_adjudications.

column.name: code
column.remarks: Type of damage. One of ELECTRICAL_REPAIR, PLUMBING_REPAIR, FURNITURE_OR_FABRIC_REPAIR, LOCK_REPAIR, REDECORATION, CLEANING, REPLACE_AN_ITEM.

column.name: details
column.remarks: Free text description of the damage.

column.name: reporter
column.remarks: DPS username of the officer who recorded this entry.

table.name: dis_issue_history
table.remarks: Full history of issuing the notice of being placed on report (DIS1/2) to the prisoner. The most recent issue is also denormalised onto reported_adjudications.

column.name: reported_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to reported_adjudications.

column.name: issuing_officer
column.remarks: DPS username of the officer who issued the form.

column.name: date_time_of_issue
column.remarks: When the form was issued to the prisoner.

table.name: draft_adjudications
table.remarks: A report being written by a reporting officer, before it is submitted for review. Rows are deleted once the draft is submitted and copied into reported_adjudications, so this table holds work in progress only and is not a historic record.

column.name: prisoner_number
column.remarks: NOMIS offender number (noms id) of the prisoner the charge is against.

column.name: incident_details_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to incident_details.

column.name: incident_statement_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to incident_statement.

column.name: originating_agency_id
column.remarks: Agency (prison) code where the incident was reported.

column.name: charge_number
column.remarks: Charge number, populated only once the draft has been submitted and a reported adjudication exists. Format is <agency><6 digit sequence>, optionally suffixed -<n> for additional charges on the same incident.

column.name: report_by_user_id
column.remarks: DPS username of the officer who submitted the report. Null while the draft is unsubmitted.

column.name: incident_role_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to incident_role.

column.name: is_youth_offender
column.remarks: True when the YOI rule set applies rather than the adult rule set. Determines which offence paragraphs and hearing types are valid.

column.name: damages_saved
column.remarks: True once the reporter has completed the damages page, including confirming there were none. Distinguishes "no damages" from "not yet asked".

column.name: evidence_saved
column.remarks: True once the reporter has completed the evidence page, including confirming there was none.

column.name: witnesses_saved
column.remarks: True once the reporter has completed the witnesses page, including confirming there were none.

column.name: gender
column.remarks: Prisoner gender, used to select the correct wording of offence paragraphs. One of MALE, FEMALE.

column.name: override_agency_id
column.remarks: Agency code the prisoner has since transferred to. Set when the report needs to be actioned by the receiving prison.

column.name: created_on_behalf_of_officer
column.remarks: Name of the officer the report was created on behalf of, when someone else entered it for them.

column.name: created_on_behalf_of_reason
column.remarks: Reason the report was created on behalf of another officer.

column.name: offender_booking_id
column.remarks: NOMIS OFFENDER_BOOK_ID for the prisoner's booking at the time of the incident.

table.name: draft_protected_characteristics
table.remarks: Protected characteristics of the victim recorded as motivating the offence, on a draft adjudication. Copied to protected_characteristics on submission.

column.name: offence_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to offence.

column.name: characteristic
column.remarks: One of AGE, DISABILITY, GENDER_REASSIGN, MARRIAGE_AND_CP, PREGNANCY_AND_MAT, RACE, RELIGION, SEX, SEX_ORIENTATION.

table.name: evidence
table.remarks: Evidence recorded on a draft adjudication. Copied to reported_evidence on submission.

column.name: draft_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to draft_adjudications.

column.name: code
column.remarks: Type of evidence. One of PHOTO, BODY_WORN_CAMERA, CCTV, BAGGED_AND_TAGGED, OTHER.

column.name: identifier
column.remarks: Reference number for the evidence, such as a body worn camera or seal number.

column.name: details
column.remarks: Free text description of the evidence.

column.name: reporter
column.remarks: DPS username of the officer who recorded this entry.

table.name: hearing
table.remarks: A scheduled or held adjudication hearing. A charge can have several hearings, for example where the first is adjourned. The ordered case history shown in the UI is assembled from this table and outcome by ReportedAdjudicationService.createOutcomeHistory().

column.name: reported_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to reported_adjudications.

column.name: agency_id
column.remarks: Agency (prison) code where the hearing takes place. May differ from the charge's originating agency after a transfer.

column.name: location_id
column.remarks: Legacy NOMIS internal location id of the hearing room. Superseded by location_uuid.

column.name: date_time_of_hearing
column.remarks: When the hearing is scheduled for, or was held.

column.name: charge_number
column.remarks: Charge number of the parent adjudication, denormalised for lookup.

column.name: oic_hearing_id
column.remarks: NOMIS OIC_HEARING_ID. Populated for migrated hearings and for hearings synchronised back to NOMIS.

column.name: oic_hearing_type
column.remarks: Who hears the case, and under which rule set. One of GOV_ADULT, GOV_YOI, INAD_ADULT, INAD_YOI, GOV. GOV = governor, INAD = independent adjudicator.

column.name: outcome_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to hearing_outcome. Null while the hearing is scheduled but not yet held.

column.name: hearing_pre_migrate_id
column.remarks: Legacy column from the NOMIS migration. The table it referenced was dropped in V91 and it is no longer populated.

column.name: representative
column.remarks: Name of the prisoner's representative at the hearing, where they had one.

column.name: location_uuid
column.remarks: Location identifier in the locations-inside-prison service. This is the current location reference.

table.name: hearing_outcome
table.remarks: What happened at a hearing. One row per hearing that has been held.

column.name: code
column.remarks: What the adjudicator did. One of COMPLETE (a finding was reached - see the linked outcome row for what it was), ADJOURN, REFER_POLICE, REFER_INAD, REFER_GOV, NOMIS (outcome was entered in NOMIS rather than DPS).

column.name: adjudicator
column.remarks: Name or username of the governor or independent adjudicator who heard the case.

column.name: adjourn_reason
column.remarks: Why the hearing was adjourned. One of LEGAL_ADVICE, LEGAL_REPRESENTATION, RO_ATTEND, HELP, UNFIT, WITNESS, WITNESS_SUPPORT, MCKENZIE, EVIDENCE, INVESTIGATION, OTHER. Null unless code is ADJOURN.

column.name: plea
column.remarks: The prisoner's plea. One of GUILTY, NOT_GUILTY, ABSTAIN, UNFIT, NOT_ASKED.

column.name: details
column.remarks: Free text notes recorded against the hearing outcome.

column.name: nomis_outcome
column.remarks: True when the outcome was recorded in NOMIS rather than DPS, so DPS holds only a placeholder.

column.name: migrated
column.remarks: True when the row was created by the NOMIS migration.

column.name: hearing_outcome_pre_migrate_id
column.remarks: Legacy column from the NOMIS migration. The table it referenced was dropped in V91 and it is no longer populated.

table.name: incident_details
table.remarks: When and where the incident happened, for a draft adjudication. One row per draft.

column.name: date_time_of_incident
column.remarks: When the incident occurred.

column.name: location_id
column.remarks: Legacy NOMIS internal location id (AGENCY_INTERNAL_LOCATIONS.INTERNAL_LOCATION_ID). Superseded by location_uuid.

column.name: handover_deadline
column.remarks: Deadline for issuing the notice of report to the prisoner, calculated as 48 hours after discovery.

column.name: date_time_of_discovery
column.remarks: When the incident was discovered. Equal to the incident date unless it was found later; the 48 hour notice clock runs from here.

column.name: location_uuid
column.remarks: Location identifier in the locations-inside-prison service. This is the current location reference.

table.name: incident_role
table.remarks: The prisoner's role in the incident for a draft adjudication - whether they committed the offence themselves or attempted, incited or assisted another. One row per draft.

column.name: role_code
column.remarks: Paragraph 25 role code. Null means the prisoner committed the offence alone. 25a = attempts to commit, 25b = incites another to commit, 25c = assists another to commit.

column.name: associated_prisoners_number
column.remarks: NOMIS offender number of the other prisoner involved, where the role code requires one.

column.name: associated_prisoners_name
column.remarks: Name of the other prisoner involved, held where they are not in the current establishment and cannot be looked up.

table.name: incident_statement
table.remarks: The reporting officer's free text account of the incident, for a draft adjudication. One row per draft.

column.name: statement
column.remarks: Free text description of the incident, up to 4000 characters.

column.name: completed
column.remarks: True once the reporter has marked the statement as finished.

table.name: offence
table.remarks: The offence selected for a draft adjudication. In practice there is one row per draft; multiple offences are raised as separate charges.

column.name: draft_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to draft_adjudications.

column.name: offence_code
column.remarks: Internal DPS offence code (an integer, not the Prison Rules paragraph). Resolved in application code by OffenceCodeLookupService against the OffenceCodes enum - there is no reference table in this database. See the reference-data export for the full code list.

column.name: victim_prisoners_number
column.remarks: NOMIS offender number of the prisoner victim, for offences that have one.

column.name: victim_staff_username
column.remarks: DPS username of the staff victim, for offences that have one.

column.name: victim_other_persons_name
column.remarks: Name of a victim who is neither a prisoner nor staff (for example a visitor).

table.name: outcome
table.remarks: A finding or decision on a charge. Some outcomes follow a hearing, others are recorded without one (for example referring to the police before any hearing). Ordering is by actual_created_date where set, otherwise create_datetime.

column.name: code
column.remarks: The finding or decision. One of REFER_POLICE, REFER_INAD, REFER_GOV, NOT_PROCEED, DISMISSED, PROSECUTION, SCHEDULE_HEARING, CHARGE_PROVED, QUASHED. CHARGE_PROVED is the outcome that allows punishments to be awarded; QUASHED revokes them.

column.name: not_proceed_reason
column.remarks: Why the charge was not proceeded with. One of ANOTHER_WAY, RELEASED, WITNESS_NOT_ATTEND, UNFIT, FLAWED, EXPIRED_NOTICE, EXPIRED_HEARING, NOT_FAIR, OTHER. Null unless code is NOT_PROCEED.

column.name: details
column.remarks: Free text supporting the outcome.

column.name: reported_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to reported_adjudications.

column.name: quashed_reason
column.remarks: Why the charge was quashed. One of FLAWED_CASE, JUDICIAL_REVIEW, APPEAL_UPHELD, OTHER. Null unless code is QUASHED.

column.name: oic_hearing_id
column.remarks: NOMIS OIC_HEARING_ID the outcome relates to, where the outcome came from or was synchronised to NOMIS.

column.name: deleted
column.remarks: Soft delete flag. Rows where this is true are excluded from the case history and from status calculation; queries must filter on "deleted is not true".

column.name: actual_created_date
column.remarks: The real date the outcome was reached, as opposed to create_datetime which for migrated records is the date of the migration run. Use this for chronology.

column.name: migrated
column.remarks: True when the row was created by the NOMIS migration.

column.name: refer_gov_reason
column.remarks: Why the case was referred to the governor. One of REVIEW_FOR_REFER_POLICE, GOV_INQUIRY, OTHER. Null unless code is REFER_GOV.

table.name: protected_characteristics
table.remarks: Protected characteristics of the victim recorded as motivating the offence, on a submitted adjudication.

column.name: reported_offence_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to reported_offence.

column.name: characteristic
column.remarks: One of AGE, DISABILITY, GENDER_REASSIGN, MARRIAGE_AND_CP, PREGNANCY_AND_MAT, RACE, RELIGION, SEX, SEX_ORIENTATION.

table.name: punishment
table.remarks: A punishment (NOMIS calls these awards or sanctions) given on a charge where the outcome was CHARGE_PROVED. The duration or amount is not held here but on the linked punishment_schedule rows. ADDITIONAL_DAYS and PROSPECTIVE_DAYS punishments are the added days that feed sentence calculation via the adjustments service.

column.name: reported_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to reported_adjudications.

column.name: type
column.remarks: Type of punishment. One of PRIVILEGE (loss of privileges - see privilege_type), EARNINGS (stoppage of earnings - see stoppage_percentage), CONFINEMENT (cellular confinement), REMOVAL_ACTIVITY, EXCLUSION_WORK, EXTRA_WORK, REMOVAL_WING, ADDITIONAL_DAYS (added days awarded, ADA), PROSPECTIVE_DAYS (prospective added days, PADA - awarded where the prisoner is not yet sentenced), CAUTION, DAMAGES_OWED (money owed for damage - see amount), PAYBACK (payback punishment, measured in hours).

column.name: privilege_type
column.remarks: Which privilege was lost. One of CANTEEN, FACILITIES, MONEY, TV, ASSOCIATION, GYM, OTHER. Null unless type is PRIVILEGE.

column.name: other_privilege
column.remarks: Free text description of the privilege lost, when privilege_type is OTHER.

column.name: stoppage_percentage
column.remarks: Percentage of earnings stopped. Null unless type is EARNINGS.

column.name: activated_by_charge_number
column.remarks: Charge number of the later adjudication that activated this suspended punishment. Null while the punishment remains suspended or was never suspended.

column.name: suspended_until
column.remarks: Date the punishment is suspended until. Non-null means the punishment is suspended and not currently in force. Mirrors the suspended_until on the latest punishment_schedule row.

column.name: sanction_seq
column.remarks: NOMIS OFFENDER_OIC_SANCTIONS.SANCTION_SEQ. With reported_adjudications.offender_booking_id this forms the composite key of the matching NOMIS sanction row.

column.name: amount
column.remarks: Amount of money owed, in pounds. Null unless type is DAMAGES_OWED.

column.name: deleted
column.remarks: Soft delete flag. Rows where this is true are excluded everywhere; queries must filter on "deleted is not true".

column.name: consecutive_to_charge_number
column.remarks: Charge number this punishment runs consecutively to. Used for added days that follow on from an award on an earlier charge.

column.name: nomis_status
column.remarks: NOMIS sanction status (reference domain OIC_SANCT_ST) for records that came from or were synchronised to NOMIS. Values include IMMEDIATE, PROSPECTIVE, SUSPENDED, SUSP_PROSP, QUASHED, AWARD_RED, REDAPP, SUSPEN_RED, SUSPEN_EXT, AS_AWARDED.

column.name: punishment_pre_migrate_id
column.remarks: Legacy column from the NOMIS migration. The table it referenced was dropped in V91 and it is no longer populated.

column.name: actual_created_date
column.remarks: The real date the punishment was awarded, as opposed to create_datetime which for migrated records is the date of the migration run. Use this for chronology.

column.name: payback_notes
column.remarks: Free text description of the payback work required. Null unless type is PAYBACK.

column.name: rehab_not_completed_outcome
column.remarks: What was decided when rehabilitative activities were not completed. One of FULL_ACTIVATE, PARTIAL_ACTIVATE, EXT_SUSPEND, NO_ACTION.

column.name: rehab_completed
column.remarks: Whether the attached rehabilitative activities were completed. Null where no rehabilitative activity applies or the outcome has not yet been recorded.

table.name: punishment_comments
table.remarks: Free text notes recorded against the punishments on a charge, including the reason for any change made on appeal or correction.

column.name: reported_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to reported_adjudications.

column.name: comment
column.remarks: The note text.

column.name: reason_for_change
column.remarks: Why the punishments were changed. One of APPEAL, CORRECTION, OTHER, GOV_OR_DIRECTOR.

column.name: nomis_created_by
column.remarks: Username of the NOMIS user who created the comment, for migrated records. Takes precedence over create_user_id when displaying the author.

column.name: actual_created_date
column.remarks: The real date the comment was made, as opposed to create_datetime which for migrated records is the date of the migration run.

table.name: punishment_schedule
table.remarks: The duration and dates of a punishment. A punishment gains a new schedule row each time it is amended, so this table is a history: take the row with the greatest create_datetime for a given punishment_fk_id to get the current position.

column.name: punishment_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to punishment.

column.name: duration
column.remarks: Length of the punishment, in the unit given by measurement. This is the added days count for ADDITIONAL_DAYS and PROSPECTIVE_DAYS punishments. Renamed from "days" in V115 when payback punishments introduced hours.

column.name: start_date
column.remarks: Date the punishment starts. Not set for added days, which take effect through a sentence adjustment rather than a date range.

column.name: end_date
column.remarks: Date the punishment ends. Not set for added days.

column.name: suspended_until
column.remarks: Date the punishment is suspended until. Non-null means the punishment was suspended as at this schedule row.

column.name: measurement
column.remarks: Unit of duration. One of DAYS, HOURS. HOURS is used only by PAYBACK punishments.

table.name: rehabilitative_activity
table.remarks: Rehabilitative activities attached to a suspended punishment as a condition of the suspension. Not available for added days, cautions, damages owed or payback punishments.

column.name: punishment_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to punishment.

column.name: details
column.remarks: Description of the activity the prisoner must complete.

column.name: monitor
column.remarks: Name of the person responsible for monitoring the activity.

column.name: end_date
column.remarks: Date the activity must be completed by.

column.name: total_sessions
column.remarks: Number of sessions the prisoner must attend.

column.name: completed
column.remarks: Whether this individual activity was completed. The overall decision is held on punishment.rehab_completed.

table.name: reported_adjudications
table.remarks: A submitted adjudication charge - the root record for everything that follows (hearings, outcomes and punishments). One row per charge, not per incident: an incident involving several prisoners, or several offences, produces several rows. Includes records migrated from NOMIS as well as those raised in DPS.

column.name: prisoner_number
column.remarks: NOMIS offender number (noms id) of the prisoner charged.

column.name: charge_number
column.remarks: Business key for the charge, unique across the service. Format is <agency><6 digit sequence> for DPS-raised charges (for example MDI001234); migrated NOMIS charges use the NOMIS charge number, suffixed -<n> where one NOMIS incident split into several DPS charges.

column.name: originating_agency_id
column.remarks: Agency (prison) code where the incident was reported. Together with override_agency_id this controls which caseload may see and action the record.

column.name: date_time_of_incident
column.remarks: When the incident occurred.

column.name: handover_deadline
column.remarks: Deadline for issuing the notice of report to the prisoner, calculated as 48 hours after discovery.

column.name: location_id
column.remarks: Legacy NOMIS internal location id where the incident occurred. Superseded by location_uuid.

column.name: statement
column.remarks: The reporting officer's free text account of the incident.

column.name: incident_role_code
column.remarks: Paragraph 25 role code. Null means the prisoner committed the offence alone. 25a = attempts to commit, 25b = incites another to commit, 25c = assists another to commit.

column.name: incident_role_associated_prisoners_number
column.remarks: NOMIS offender number of the other prisoner involved, where the role code requires one.

column.name: status
column.remarks: Current state of the charge. One of AWAITING_REVIEW, RETURNED, REJECTED, ACCEPTED (deprecated), UNSCHEDULED, SCHEDULED, ADJOURNED, REFER_POLICE, REFER_INAD, REFER_GOV, PROSECUTION, DISMISSED, NOT_PROCEED, CHARGE_PROVED, QUASHED, INVALID_OUTCOME, INVALID_SUSPENDED, INVALID_ADA. Derived from the latest outcome by ReportedAdjudication.calculateStatus(). The three INVALID_* values flag data inconsistencies inherited from the NOMIS migration rather than real states.

column.name: status_reason
column.remarks: Reason code recorded when the reviewer rejected or returned the report.

column.name: status_details
column.remarks: Free text the reviewer gave alongside status_reason.

column.name: is_youth_offender
column.remarks: True when the YOI rule set applies rather than the adult rule set.

column.name: review_user_id
column.remarks: DPS username of the reviewer who accepted, rejected or returned the report.

column.name: incident_role_associated_prisoners_name
column.remarks: Name of the other prisoner involved, held where they cannot be looked up.

column.name: date_time_of_discovery
column.remarks: When the incident was discovered. Equal to the incident date unless it was found later; the 48 hour notice clock runs from here.

column.name: gender
column.remarks: Prisoner gender, used to select the correct wording of offence paragraphs. One of MALE, FEMALE.

column.name: issuing_officer
column.remarks: DPS username of the officer who most recently issued the notice of report (DIS1/2) to the prisoner.

column.name: date_time_of_issue
column.remarks: When the notice of report was most recently issued. Full history is in dis_issue_history.

column.name: date_time_of_first_hearing
column.remarks: Denormalised date of the earliest hearing, maintained for reporting and sorting.

column.name: override_agency_id
column.remarks: Agency code the prisoner transferred to after the charge was raised, set by TransferService from prisoner movement events. Null when there has been no transfer.

column.name: last_modified_agency_id
column.remarks: Agency code of the establishment that last changed the record.

column.name: agency_incident_id
column.remarks: NOMIS AGENCY_INCIDENT_ID the charge came from. Populated on migrated records and on records synchronised back to NOMIS.

column.name: migrated
column.remarks: True when the record was migrated from NOMIS rather than raised in DPS.

column.name: offender_booking_id
column.remarks: NOMIS OFFENDER_BOOK_ID for the booking the charge belongs to. Use this to join to NOMIS sentence and sanction data.

column.name: status_before_migration
column.remarks: Status the charge held in NOMIS before migration. Populated on migrated records only.

column.name: created_on_behalf_of_officer
column.remarks: Name of the officer the report was created on behalf of, when someone else entered it for them.

column.name: created_on_behalf_of_reason
column.remarks: Reason the report was created on behalf of another officer.

column.name: migrated_inactive_prisoner
column.remarks: True when the record was migrated for a prisoner who was no longer active. These records are excluded from the INVALID_* status checks because their data cannot be corrected.

column.name: migrated_split_record
column.remarks: True when a single NOMIS charge was split into multiple DPS charges during migration.

column.name: date_time_resubmitted
column.remarks: When a returned report was resubmitted for review. Where set, this is treated as the report's created date in the API.

column.name: location_uuid
column.remarks: Location identifier in the locations-inside-prison service. This is the current location reference.

table.name: reported_damages
table.remarks: Damage to prison property recorded on a submitted adjudication.

column.name: reported_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to reported_adjudications.

column.name: code
column.remarks: Type of damage. One of ELECTRICAL_REPAIR, PLUMBING_REPAIR, FURNITURE_OR_FABRIC_REPAIR, LOCK_REPAIR, REDECORATION, CLEANING, REPLACE_AN_ITEM.

column.name: details
column.remarks: Free text description of the damage.

column.name: reporter
column.remarks: DPS username of the officer who recorded this entry.

column.name: repair_cost
column.remarks: Cost of repair in pounds, where recorded. Separate from the DAMAGES_OWED punishment, which is the amount the prisoner was ordered to pay.

table.name: reported_evidence
table.remarks: Evidence recorded on a submitted adjudication.

column.name: reported_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to reported_adjudications.

column.name: code
column.remarks: Type of evidence. One of PHOTO, BODY_WORN_CAMERA, CCTV, BAGGED_AND_TAGGED, OTHER.

column.name: identifier
column.remarks: Reference number for the evidence, such as a body worn camera or seal number.

column.name: details
column.remarks: Free text description of the evidence.

column.name: reporter
column.remarks: DPS username of the officer who recorded this entry.

column.name: date_added
column.remarks: When the evidence was added to the report, where it was added after submission.

table.name: reported_offence
table.remarks: The offence charged on a submitted adjudication. One row per reported adjudication.

column.name: reported_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to reported_adjudications.

column.name: offence_code
column.remarks: Internal DPS offence code (an integer, not the Prison Rules paragraph). Resolved in application code by OffenceCodeLookupService against the OffenceCodes enum - there is no reference table in this database. Migrated NOMIS charges that could not be mapped use the MIGRATED_OFFENCE code and carry the original text in nomis_offence_code / nomis_offence_description. See the reference-data export for the full code list.

column.name: victim_prisoners_number
column.remarks: NOMIS offender number of the prisoner victim, for offences that have one.

column.name: victim_staff_username
column.remarks: DPS username of the staff victim, for offences that have one.

column.name: victim_other_persons_name
column.remarks: Name of a victim who is neither a prisoner nor staff (for example a visitor).

column.name: nomis_offence_code
column.remarks: NOMIS OIC offence code, for example 51:1A. Populated on migrated records.

column.name: nomis_offence_description
column.remarks: NOMIS offence description text. Populated on migrated records, and used in place of the DPS paragraph description where the offence could not be mapped.

column.name: migrated
column.remarks: True when the offence row was created by the NOMIS migration.

column.name: actual_offence_code
column.remarks: The offence code originally chosen, retained when the stored offence_code was subsequently adjusted.

table.name: reported_witness
table.remarks: Witnesses recorded on a submitted adjudication.

column.name: reported_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to reported_adjudications.

column.name: code
column.remarks: Type of witness. One of OFFICER, STAFF, OTHER_PERSON, VICTIM, PRISONER.

column.name: first_name
column.remarks: Witness first name.

column.name: last_name
column.remarks: Witness last name.

column.name: reporter
column.remarks: DPS username of the officer who recorded this entry.

column.name: comment
column.remarks: Free text note about the witness or their evidence.

column.name: username
column.remarks: DPS username of the witness where they are a member of staff.

column.name: date_added
column.remarks: When the witness was added to the report, where they were added after submission.

table.name: witness
table.remarks: Witnesses recorded on a draft adjudication. Copied to reported_witness on submission.

column.name: draft_adjudication_fk_id
column.remarks: Foreign key to draft_adjudications.

column.name: code
column.remarks: Type of witness. One of OFFICER, STAFF, OTHER_PERSON, VICTIM, PRISONER.

column.name: first_name
column.remarks: Witness first name.

column.name: last_name
column.remarks: Witness last name.

column.name: reporter
column.remarks: DPS username of the officer who recorded this entry.

column.name: username
column.remarks: DPS username of the witness where they are a member of staff.
